title: 10 best herbal teas for strep throat

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with strep throat because it contains compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate the soreness and discomfort associated with this condition. The tea's flavonoids and glycosides also have antibacterial properties, which can aid in fighting off the Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria that causes strep throat. Additionally, licorice teas' soothing qualities can provide relief from a scratchy throat and cough, promoting a more comfortable recovery.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has antibacterial properties, which can help combat the Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria that cause strep throat.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: These bioactive compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the throat and alleviate symptoms such as soreness and discomfort.
Liquiritin and isoliquiritigenin: These compounds have been shown to have antimicrobial activity against Streptococcus pyogenes, making them effective against strep throat infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm sore throats, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by strep throat.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain triterpenoids, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections like strep throat.
remedy_recipe: Gather organic glycyrrhiza glabra roots dried and crushed into fine powder.
Measure 1 teaspoon of the powdered root for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the mixture in a teapot or infuser for 5 to 7 minutes allowing flavors to meld.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding solids and residue.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then consume immediately within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower teas helps with strep throat because of its natural antibacterial properties, specifically targeting the bacteria that causes strep throat. The tea contains compounds like cinnamic acid and rosmarinic acid, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus pyogenes, the bacterial strain responsible for most cases of strep throat. Additionally, coneflower's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the throat, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating symptoms of strep throat.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Kansas Coneflower tea contains antimicrobial compounds like echinacin, which help combat bacterial and viral infections, including streptococcus pyogenes, the bacteria responsible for strep throat.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Kansas Coneflower tea have anti-inflammatory effects, reducing swelling and pain associated with strep throat inflammation and infection.

Immune system support: Kansas Coneflower tea is believed to stimulate the immune system's natural response, enhancing the body's ability to fight off infections like strep throat by increasing white blood cell production and activity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial compounds, making it effective in fighting against bacterial infections like strep throat.
Barks: Containing tannins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe a sore throat.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried echinacea angustifolia root and pure mountain spring water in a medium-sized teapot.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried echinacea root for every eight ounces of water.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes to allow the herbs to release their beneficial properties slowly.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any loose particles.
Drink the warm tea three times daily, allowing it to cool slightly before consumption.

Leaves: Sage leaves contain salviarin, a compound that exhibits antibacterial properties, making them effective against strep throat infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried salvia officinalis leaves from a trusted source measuring one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Heat filtered water in a teapot or kettle to a rolling boil for three to five minutes.
Steep the dried herbs in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes allowing the flavors and properties to infuse.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth discarding the solids after one minute.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint teas helps with strep throat because it contains natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and discomfort. The menthol present in peppermint tea also helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, which can aid in clearing out infected bacteria. remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria, which causes strep throat, and soothe the affected areas.

remedy_recipe: Harvest one cup of fresh peppermint leaves or use dried peppermint flakes for brewing.
Combine the harvested mint leaves or dried peppermint flakes with one quart of boiling water in a large pot.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the menthol and antibacterial properties.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a large glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Store the prepared peppermint tea in the refrigerator for up to three days or freeze it for later use as needed.

Drink the tea hot, three times a day, for up to five days as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild indigo teas helps with strep throat because it contains a potent antimicrobial compound called berberine, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria that cause the infection. The tea's antiviral properties also help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as sore throats, fever, and swollen lymph nodes. Additionally, indigo's antioxidant properties may help to boost the immune system, allowing it to recover more quickly from the illness.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wild indigo tea has been shown to inhibit the growth of Streptococcus pyogenes, the bacteria that causes strep throat, which helps to reduce the severity and duration of the infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in wild indigo tea have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the throat, as well as alleviate discomfort caused by the infection.

Immune-boosting properties: Wild indigo tea contains compounds that stimulate the immune system, which can help to increase the body's natural defense against infections like strep throat, reducing the risk of complications and promoting faster recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used in medicinal teas for strep throat due to their antibacterial properties, which help combat the infection.
Barks: The barks contain a range of bioactive compounds that contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making them effective in soothing sore throats.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried baptisia tinctoria leaves and stems.
Measure one teaspoon of the herb per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for seven to ten minutes, then strain.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before drinking it.
Drink the tea three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain teas helps with strep throat because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tea's active compounds, such as allantoin and berberine, have been shown to soothe sore throats and reduce inflammation caused by the streptococcus bacteria. Additionally, plantain's antimicrobial properties help combat the infection, speeding up the healing process. By drinking plantain tea, individuals with strep throat can find relief from discomfort, pain, and fever, allowing their bodies to recover more efficiently.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling in the throat and alleviate discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds present in plantain tea, including triterpenoids and saponins, help combat the Streptococcus pyogenes bacteria that cause strep throat, reducing the risk of infection and speeding up recovery.

Barks: The barks of the plantain plant contain tannins, which have antibacterial properties that can help combat strep throat bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one cup of fresh plantago major leaves and flowers and rinse them gently with cold water.
Combine the rinsed plant material with two cups of boiling water in a large pot.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the tea.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean cup and discard the solids.
Add one teaspoon of raw honey and stir well before serving hot within 30-40 minutes of preparation.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat strep throat with herbal teas is to combine a soothing tea blend of slippery elm, marshmallow root, and licorice root to calm the throat irritation. supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to strep throat that are treated with herbal teas are pharyngitis, tonsillitis, and laryngitis. Herbal teas such as slippery elm, licorice root, and marshmallow root soothe sore throats and reduce inflammation. Yerba mate tea has antimicrobial properties that help combat bacterial infections, while eucalyptus and peppermint teas provide decongestant relief for congested sinuses and a scratchy throat. intro_desc: Herbal teas for strep throat are a natural remedy that can help alleviate the symptoms of strep throat, such as soreness, inflammation, and fever. These teas contain antiviral and antibacterial properties that help combat the infection, promoting healing and relief. Examples of beneficial herbal teas include peppermint, chamomile, and slippery elm, which can be consumed hot or iced to ease discomfort and promote relaxation, improving quality of life for those affected by strep throat.
